message:
Tighten env-var parsing and isolate backup-dir tests

Two related cleanups that both came out of the same review pass over
frankensearch-core config handling and the fsfs rollback/backup tests:

Config: parse_env_bool helper
--------------------------------
Previously TwoTierConfig::apply_env_overrides treated the boolean
environment variables FRANKENSEARCH_FAST_ONLY and
FRANKENSEARCH_GRAPH_RANKING_ENABLED with `val == "true" || val == "1"`,
which meant:

- Common spellings like `yes`, `on`, `TRUE`, `FALSE`, ` off ` silently
  mapped to false, so a user thinking "I turned it on" would actually
  disable the feature.
- Typos like `enable` or stray whitespace produced `false` with no
  signal that anything was wrong.
- There was no symmetric "false/off/no/0" set, so the override could
  not reliably *disable* a flag that defaulted to true.

Added TwoTierConfig::parse_env_bool(&str) which:

- Trims whitespace, rejects empty string.
- Matches {true,1,yes,on} and {false,0,no,off} case-insensitively.
- Returns Option so apply_env_overrides can emit a tracing::warn! with
  the variable name and offending value when the value is unknown,
  keeping the existing default rather than silently flipping it.

Unit tests cover truthy, falsey, whitespace-padded, and rejected
values. The same helper is now used for both flags and should be
extended to any future boolean env overrides.

Runtime: backup-dir isolation for tests
--------------------------------
The rollback/backup tests for fsfs were all sharing the real
dirs::data_dir()/frankensearch/backups directory:

- list_backups_returns_vec, write_and_read_rollback_manifest_roundtrip,
  create_backup_produces_valid_entry,
  create_backup_deduplicates_same_version, and
  restore_backup_fails_with_no_backups all wrote manifests into the
  developer's XDG data dir.
- That directory is shared with the installed fsfs on the same machine
  and with every other test invocation, which made the dedup assertion
  flaky ("count == 1") depending on whether a real install had been
  running, and left rollback entries sitting in ~/.local/share after
  test runs.

This commit introduces a thread-local `BACKUP_DIR_OVERRIDE` guarded by
`#[cfg(test)]` and honored by `backup_dir()`. A new test helper
`with_test_backup_dir(label, |dir| ...)` creates a unique temp
directory, installs it as the override for the duration of the
closure, runs the test, then removes the temp dir on the way out. The
override is restored on the thread-local so parallel tests do not
stomp each other. Each of the five tests above now runs inside
`with_test_backup_dir(...)`, which:

- Gives every test its own fresh manifest (fixes dedup flake).
- Stops leaving stale backup dirs in the user's XDG data dir.
- Makes the `create_backup_*` tests fully deterministic.

Renames
-------
`normalize_model_token` is renamed to `normalize_model_key` at its
three call sites + definition. "Token" was misleading because the
function is used as a hash key for manifest matching (id/install/
display/repo), not as a tokenization step for search. Pure rename, no
behavior change.

Miscellaneous
-------------
- The broken-binary self-test was matching on `verify` with a
  pattern that reached for `ok() if out.status.success()` on the
  success arm and then panicked. Flattened to `expect` + boolean
  asserts so the test reads top-to-bottom.
- The oversized-request test now uses `matches!` instead of a match
  arm that only extracts the field, so the failure message includes
  the full error on mismatch.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
